issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is invalid, the code does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input, so screen readers do not convey the invalid state, violating WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not manage focus when opened
mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger

Screen reader users will not be informed whether the navigation menu is expanded or collapsed, breaking the accessibility of the toggle.

Line 2010: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`. Line 2015: ensure the toggle function correctly sets the attribute based on the new state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.fix: Line 2150: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 2160: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open and close functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce dynamic results to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product grid updates, no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that the content has changed, leaving them unaware of new results.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` rule removes the outline (`outline: none`) and does not provide any visible alternative (e.g., box‑shadow or colored border). Keyboard users lose a focus indicator on all interactive elements.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule strips the default focus outline for elements that are not `:focus-visible`, leaving no visible focus cue for keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons lose their outline on `:focus-visible` and no alternative visual indicator (e.g., box‑shadow) is supplied, breaking WCAG 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter‑tab buttons have their outline removed on `:focus-visible` without any replacement, making keyboard focus invisible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ toggle buttons strip the outline on `:focus-visible` and provide no alternative visual cue, violating focus visibility requirements.

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .nav-toggle (min‑height/min‑width 28 px)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button has a minimum height and width of 28 px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 44 × 44 px, making it hard to tap.

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .btn (min‑height 32 px)
issuesFound.explanation: Base button class sets `min-height: 32px`, below the recommended 44 px target size, reducing tapability.

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .btn-sm (min‑height 20 px)
issuesFound.explanation: Small button variant defines `min-height: 20px`, far below the 44 px minimum, creating a very small tap target.

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .btn-lg (min‑height 36 px)
issuesFound.explanation: Large button variant sets `min-height: 36px`, still below the 44 px minimum target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .btn-icon (min‑width/min‑height 20 px)
issuesFound.explanation: Icon‑only buttons have a minimum size of 20 px, which is well under the 44 px target size, making them difficult to activate by touch.

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .filter-tabs button (min‑height 28 px)
issuesFound.explanation: Filter‑tab buttons have `min-height: 28px`, below the 44 px minimum, reducing accessibility for touch users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .pricing-toggle button (min‑height 28 px)
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle buttons use `min-height: 28px`, which does not meet the 44 px minimum target size.

issuesFound.title: Visually hidden utility (.sr-only) removes content from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, `height: auto`, and `overflow: visible`, which does **not** hide the element from visual layout but also fails to hide it from assistive technologies correctly; it does not use the required off‑screen technique and may expose hidden content to AT.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-image img` animates `transform` with a non‑zero duration, but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block for this selector is empty, so users who prefer reduced motion still experience the animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover (translateY)
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state of the primary CTA button applies `transform: translateY(-1px)`, creating movement without a reduced‑motion fallback.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes the default underline from all links, and no other visual differentiator (such as color contrast, border, or background) is provided, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text for many users.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav trigger toggles the drawer via open() and close() but never sets aria‑expanded on the button. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the navigation menu is open, violating ARIA state requirements.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The element [data‑billing‑toggle] toggles between monthly and annual billing, but its aria‑pressed state is never changed, leaving the on/off status invisible to assistive tech.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: markInvalid() adds describedby/error text but never sets aria‑invalid, so screen readers cannot detect that a field is in an error state.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: markValid() removes the error message but leaves aria‑invalid on the field, causing a persistent false‑error state for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count is not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible count badge is updated but no ARIA live region is used, so screen‑reader users are unaware of how many products are shown.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period change does not announce the new period to AT
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the pricing period updates UI elements but no ARIA live region is used, so users of assistive technology do not hear that the period switched.

issuesFound.title: View‑mode toggle does not announce the new layout to AT
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between grid and list view changes CSS classes only; no live region informs screen‑reader users of the layout change.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: In **pricing**, the billing‑period buttons (`_periodBtns`) are toggled with CSS classes only; no `aria‑pressed` state is set, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the selected period.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_syncToggleState(period)` add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', btn.getAttribute('data-period') === period ? 'true' : 'false');` for each button, and initialise the attribute on page load.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In **dropdownMenu**, the trigger button’s visual state is toggled, but the code never sets `aria‑expanded` to reflect whether the submenu is open.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_openInstance(inst)` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `_closeInstance(inst)` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In **faqAccordion**, each FAQ item’s trigger button is given `aria‑controls`, but the `aria‑expanded` state is never set when an item is opened or closed, breaking the ARIA accordion pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
In `open(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `close(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Search combobox input lacks aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In **siteSearch**, the search input is given `role="combobox"` and `aria‑autocomplete="list"` but never receives `aria‑expanded` to indicate when the suggestion list is visible.
issuesFound.fix:
When the suggestion panel is opened (`_open()`), call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and set it to `false` when the panel is closed.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator never sets aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The **inlineValidator**’s `_renderResult` adds error text and CSS classes but does **not** set `aria‑invalid="true"` on fields that fail validation, so screen readers do not learn that the field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_renderResult` when `!result.valid` add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` and when `result.valid` remove the attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not clear aria‑invalid on valid input (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: While the validator adds `aria‑invalid` for errors (see Issue 6), it never removes the attribute when the field becomes valid again, leaving stale invalid state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_renderResult` when `result.valid` call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.

---

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load for mobile‑nav trigger (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav trigger button is never given an initial `aria‑expanded="false"` value, violating the requirement that hidden widgets expose their collapsed state on load.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.init()` after locating `_trigger`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count is not announced to screen readers (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The badge showing the number of matching products is updated visually but no live‑region announcement is made, so users of assistive technology are unaware of result‑set changes.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the badge text, call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} found`);` or add an `aria-live="polite"` element that receives the count.

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period change does not announce the new period (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: When the user switches between monthly/quarterly/annual billing, the UI updates the price labels but no ARIA live region is used to announce the change, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the new period.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the UI, add `liveRegion.announce(`Billing period switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`);` or update a hidden `role="status"` element with the same text.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required field marker announces bare asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The visual required field marker (*) is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce "star" separately from the "(required)" text that follows.
issuesFound.fix: Line 823: *
